nodes.title: The Discovery Flow: From Problem to Experiments

David Pereira

Simplifying Product Management

d-pereira.com
An app that evaluates how healthy your meal is
User takes a picture, app gives an evaluation and suggestion to make it better
People striving to become healthy and understand how their meals contribute to their goals

1. Describe the problem you want to solve for which audience, the solution you're considering, and how the user will interact with it.
  2. Keep it succint.
  3. See the example below.
This experiment aims to help you assess the healthiness of your meals and make better nutritional choices.
Join 75-100 participants in this 1-week experiment to improve your nutrition awareness
Take a clear photo of your meal to receive a healthiness score
How to take a good photo:
Tap to take a photo or upload from gallery
Your photo will be analyzed to provide nutritional insights
Your meal has a high healthiness score
You're checking your meal scores 40% more than the average user! This consistent engagement is helping you stay on track.
Your healthiness scores have improved by 0.8 points since you started tracking regularly.
Try logging breakfast meals more consistently to improve your overall nutrition balance.
From idea to prototype in minutes, not weeks.
Get suggestions of multiple experiments, from 4 hours to a week

Intructions:

  1. Run the assumption flow to identify how to de-risk your idea
